title: sendAndConfirmTransaction with commitment: "processed" — a transaction that can still be rolled back is treated as final

params.passDetail: Confirmation waits for a durable commitment (not "processed") — the transaction is only treated as done once it is confirmed/finalized and cannot be dropped.
params.failDetail: sendAndConfirmTransaction is called with commitment: "processed", the weakest level: the transaction has merely been processed by the current leader and can still be dropped or rolled back if that fork does not win. Code that unlocks a purchase, credits a balance, or ships goods on this 'confirmation' acts on a state that may never finalize — a double-spend / phantom-payment window. Use "confirmed" or "finalized" before treating a transaction as settled.
params.absentCallDetail: No sendAndConfirmTransaction call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: sendAndConfirmTransaction does not set commitment; verify the resolved commitment is confirmed/finalized, not processed.

provenance.note: commitment: "processed" is chosen for snappy UX and then reused as the settlement signal. It reads as 'confirmed' to the author but is the weakest, reversible level — the phantom-payment footgun the unconfirmed-state class captures.
